what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment metadata record that links an attachment identifier to a board item in the dragnet-development channel.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
The target is a URI using the board:// scheme: board://item-f17968de.
It records an added timestamp in ISO 8601 format with a UTC offset.
There are no code symbols or explanatory notes inside the file.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a fixed set of metadata fields: id, channel, attachment_type, target, added.
References an external board item via a board:// URI.
concerns: The file contains no body or description, so the nature of the attachment is not self-evident.
The target board item cannot be resolved or understood from this file alone.
No author or provenance information is recorded.
notes: A bare metadata stub that ties attachment att-6475533d to board item f17968de in the dragnet-development channel.
questions: What content or artifact does this attachment represent?
What is the board item f17968de and what does it contain?
What does the attachment_type 'board_items' imply about the relationship?
Who added this attachment and why?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to be an attachment linkage record, and its content matches that purpose.
intention_signals: It seems intended to create a durable, retrievable association between a channel attachment and a specific board item for later lookup or context.
novelty: The board:// URI scheme for targeting a board item is a notable convention.
contribution: It contributes a persistent typed link between an attachment identifier and a board item, plus a creation timestamp, which a fused system would keep as a structural reference.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML-frontmatter channel attachment record (id att-6475533d) of type board_items for channel dragnet-development, targeting board://item-f17968de and recording an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to link an attachment to a specific board item in a development channel.
